Uses of Class
com.irurueta.units.TemperatureUnit

    • Uses of TemperatureUnit in com.irurueta.units

      Methods in com.irurueta.units that return TemperatureUnit 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      TemperatureUnit TemperatureFormatter.findUnit​(java.lang.String source)
      Attempts to determine a temperature unit within a measurement string representation.
      static TemperatureUnit[] TemperatureUnit.getImperialUnits()
      Gets all supported imperial temperature units.
      static TemperatureUnit[] TemperatureUnit.getMetricUnits()
      Gets all supported metric temperature units.
      static TemperatureUnit TemperatureUnit.valueOf​(java.lang.String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name.
      static TemperatureUnit[] TemperatureUnit.values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared.
      Methods in com.irurueta.units with parameters of type TemperatureUnit 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void Temperature.add​(double value, TemperatureUnit unit)
      Adds provided temperature value and unit and updates current temperature instance.
      static double Temperature.add​(double value1, TemperatureUnit unit1, double value2, TemperatureUnit unit2, TemperatureUnit resultUnit)
      Adds two temperature values and units and returns the result.
      void Temperature.add​(java.lang.Number value, TemperatureUnit unit)
      Adds provided temperature value and unit and updates current temperature instance.
      static java.lang.Number Temperature.add​(java.lang.Number value1, TemperatureUnit unit1, java.lang.Number value2, TemperatureUnit unit2, TemperatureUnit resultUnit)
      Adds two temperature values and units and returns the result.
      Temperature Temperature.addAndReturnNew​(double value, TemperatureUnit unit, TemperatureUnit resultUnit)
      Adds provided temperature value and unit and returns a new temperature instance using provided unit.
      static Temperature Temperature.addAndReturnNew​(Temperature arg1, Temperature arg2, TemperatureUnit unit)
      Adds two temperature instances.
      Temperature Temperature.addAndReturnNew​(Temperature t, TemperatureUnit unit)
      Adds provided temperature to current instance and returns a new temperature instance.
      Temperature Temperature.addAndReturnNew​(java.lang.Number value, TemperatureUnit unit, TemperatureUnit resultUnit)
      Adds provided temperature value and unit and returns a new temperature instance using provided unit.
      static double TemperatureConverter.convert​(double input, TemperatureUnit inputUnit, TemperatureUnit outputUnit)
      Converts a temperature value from input unit to provided output unit.
      static void TemperatureConverter.convert​(Temperature temperature, TemperatureUnit outputUnit)
      Converts and updates a temperature to requested output unit.
      static void TemperatureConverter.convert​(Temperature input, TemperatureUnit outputUnit, Temperature result)
      Converts a temperature to requested output unit.
      static java.lang.Number TemperatureConverter.convert​(java.lang.Number input, TemperatureUnit inputUnit, TemperatureUnit outputUnit)
      Converts a temperature value from input unit to provided output unit.
      static Temperature TemperatureConverter.convertAndReturnNew​(Temperature input, TemperatureUnit outputUnit)
      Converts a temperature to requested output unit.
      java.lang.String TemperatureFormatter.formatAndConvert​(java.lang.Number value, TemperatureUnit unit, UnitSystem system)
      Formats and converts provided temperature value and unit using provided unit system.
      java.lang.String TemperatureFormatter.getUnitSymbol​(TemperatureUnit unit)
      Returns unit string representation.
      static UnitSystem TemperatureUnit.getUnitSystem​(TemperatureUnit unit)
      Returns unit system for provided temperature unit.
      static boolean TemperatureUnit.isImperial​(TemperatureUnit unit)
      Indicates whether provided unit belongs to the imperial unit system.
      static boolean TemperatureUnit.isMetric​(TemperatureUnit unit)
      Indicates whether provided unit belongs to the metric unit system.
      void Temperature.subtract​(double value, TemperatureUnit unit)
      Subtracts provided temperature value and unit and updates current temperature instance.

      Subtracts two temperature values and unit and returns the result.

      Subtracts provided temperature value and unit and updates current temperature instance.

      Subtracts two temperature values and units and returns the result.

      Subtracts provided temperature value and unit and returns a new temperature instance using provided unit.

      Subtracts two temperature instances.

      Subtracts provided temperature from current instance and returns a new instance.

      Subtracts provided temperature value and unit and returns a new temperature instance using provided unit.
